# Instagram Post — NVIDIA's $2B CoreWeave Investment and the AI Infrastructure Arms Race Hitting Gigawatt Scale
# Week: Week of 2026-03-09

---

AI infrastructure just entered the gigawatt era.

Forget server counts. The new unit of AI competition is raw power capacity — and the numbers from this week are staggering.

NVIDIA invested $2 billion in CoreWeave to build toward 5 gigawatts of AI compute factories by 2030. CoreWeave's contracted revenue backlog reached $66.8 billion by end of 2025. Meta and AMD locked in a 5-year partnership for up to 6 gigawatts of GPU capacity.

This is not incremental growth. This is a fundamental shift in how enterprises must think about AI readiness. Compute is becoming the new capital asset — as strategic as supply chains, as critical as manufacturing capacity.

For CEOs and CTOs, the question is no longer whether to invest in AI infrastructure. It is whether your current compute strategy can compete at this scale. Single-vendor reliance is becoming a risk. Multi-cloud, dedicated-capacity strategies are becoming the baseline for serious AI deployment.

The organizations securing compute access now will set the pace for the next five years.

GYanTech AI helps leaders build AI infrastructure strategies that match this moment. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- NVIDIA invested $2 billion in CoreWeave in January 2026 to accelerate buildout toward 5 gigawatts of AI factories by 2030 (Source: The New Stack, AI Cloud Taxonomy 2026)
- CoreWeave reported a contracted revenue backlog of $66.8 billion as of December 31, 2025, up from $55.6 billion at end of Q3 2025 (Source: The New Stack, AI Cloud Taxonomy 2026)
- Meta and AMD announced a 5-year partnership on Feb 24, 2026, with AMD supplying up to 6 gigawatts of Instinct GPUs (Source: devFlokers, AI Breakthroughs March 2026)
- CoreWeave reached more than 850 megawatts of connected capacity (Source: The New Stack, AI Cloud Taxonomy 2026)
